title: Transformer with a winding arrangemnet of litz wires
abstract: A transformer (100), particularly a medium frequency transformer, is described. The transformer (100) includes a transformer core having a core leg with a longitudinal axis (111), and a winding arrangement (120) including a conductor (125) wound around the longitudinal axis (111) of the core leg with a number of turns N t, wherein the conductor (125) includes a group of litz wires which are electrically connected in parallel. The group of litz wires (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6) includes a first subgroup of litz wires (1, 2, 3) and a second subgroup of litz wires (4, 5, 6). The winding arrangement (120) includes at least one transposition (160) in which a radial position of the first subgroup of litz wires (1, 2, 3) and a radial position of the second subgroup litz wires (4, 5, 6) are interchanged.
claims: Claims 1. A transformer (100), particularly a middle frequency transformer, comprising: - a transformer core having a core leg with a longitudinal axis (111), and - a winding arrangement ( 120) comprising a conductor (125) wound around the longitudinal axis (111) of the core leg with a number of turns Nt, wherein the conductor (125) comprises a group of litz wires (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6) which are electrically connected in parallel, wherein the group of litz wires comprises a first subgroup of litz wires (1 , 2, 3) and a second subgroup of litz wires (4, 5, 6), and wherein the winding arrangement (120), in particular the conductor (125), comprises at least one transposition (160) in which a radial position of the first subgroup of litz wires (1, 2, 3) and a radial position of the second subgroup litz wires (4, 5, 6) are interchanged. 2. The transformer (100) according to claim 1, wherein the transposition (160) is located after the half of the number of turns Nt of the conductor (125) being wound around the longitudinal axis (111) of the core leg. 3. The transformer (100) according to any one of the claims 1 to 2, wherein a radial position of one litz wire of the first subgroup of litz wires (1, 2, 3) and a radial position of one litz wire of the second subgroup of litz wires (4, 5, 6) are transposed after an integer number of turns of the conductor ( 125) being wound around the longitudinal axis (111) of the core leg. The transformer (100) according to any one of the claims 1 to 3, wherein a radial position of one litz wire of the first subgroup of litz wires (1, 2, 3) and a radial position of one litz wire of the second subgroup of litz wires (4, 5, 6) are transposed after each turn of the conductor (125) wound around the longitudinal axis (111) of the core leg. The transformer (100) according to any one of the claims 1 to 4, wherein a radial position of one litz wire of the first subgroup of litz wires (1, 2, 3) and a radial position of one litz wire of the second subgroup of litz wires (4, 5, 6) are transposed according to a Roebel transposition. The transformer (100) according to any one of the claims 1 to 2, wherein the winding arrangement (120) includes at least one transposition in which radial positions of two litz wires of the first subgroup of litz wires (1 , 2, 3) and radial positions of two litz wires of the second subgroup of litz wires (4, 5, 6) are changed in a radial direction, which is defined relative to the longitudinal axis (111) of the core leg, according to a Roebel transposition. The transformer (100) according to any one of the claims 1 to 2, wherein radial positions of two litz wires of the first subgroup of litz wires (1, 2, 3) and radial positions of two litz wires of the second subgroup of litz wires (4, 5, 6) are changed in a radial direction relative to the longitudinal axis (1 11) of the core leg according to a Roebel transposition after an integer number of turns of the conductor (125) being wound around the longitudinal axis (111) of the core leg, or after each turn of the conductor (125) being wound around the longitudinal axis (111) of the core leg. 8. The transformer (100) according to any one of the claims 1 to 7, wherein the transpositions (160) divide the length of the conductor wound around the core leg into a number of sections that are of essentially equal lengths along the longitudinal axis (1 1 1), in particular that are of exactly equal lengths along the longitudinal axis (1 1 1), wherein the number of sections corresponds to the number Nw of litz wires (1 , 2, 3, 4, 5, 6) of the conductor (125). 9. The transformer (100) according to any one of the claims 1 to 8, wherein the number of turns Nt of the conductor (125) wound around the longitudinal axis (1 1 1) of the core leg divided by the number Nw of litz wires (1 , 2, 3, 4, 5, 6) of the conductor (125) is an integer, and wherein the number of transpositions M provided in the winding arrangement (120) is one less than the number Nw of litz wires (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6) included in the group of litz wires (1 , 2, 3, 4, 5, 6) of the conductor (125). 10. The transformer (100) according to any one of the claims 1 to 9, wherein the transpositions (160) are located in a transposition zone (128) between a circumferential position of a conductor inlet (126) and a circumferential position of a conductor outlet (127), in particular in that the transition zone (128) includes Nt-1 turns. 1 1. The transformer (100) according any one of the claims 1 to 10, wherein the conductor (125) has an essentially rectangular cross-section, in particular wherein each litz wire (1 , 2, 3, 4, 5, 6) of the conductor (125) or of the group of litz wires (1 , 2, 3, 4, 5, 6), which are electricall connected in parallel, has a rectangular or quadratic cross-section. 12. The transformer (100) according to any one of the claims 1 to 1 1, wherein the winding arrangement (120) is incorporated in a casting. 13. The transformer (100) according to any one of the claims 1 to 12, wherein the transformer (100) is a medium frequency transformer (100), in particular wherein the medium frequency transformer (100) is operated in a frequency range above 1 kHz, in particular a range of 10 kHz. 14. The transformer (100) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the transformer (100) is provided with a winding arrangement having all transpositions (160) at the same circumferential position. 15. The transformer (100) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the conductor (125) is wound from a first axial end (112) of a cylindrical element, along the longitudinal axis (111) of the transformer core leg, to a second axial end (113) of the cylindrical element, and a distance D provided between the first end (112) and the second end (113) is D=(Nt Ï Na x h) + M x h, wherein Nt is the total number of turns of the conductor around the longitudinal axis (111) of the core leg, Na is the number of litz wires (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6) provided within the conductor (125) in the direction of the longitudinal axis (111), h is the height of a litz wire (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6), and M is the number of transpositions. 16. The transformer (100) according to any one of the claims 1 to 8 and 10 to 15, wherein the number of turns Nt of the conductor (125) wound around the longitudinal axis (11 1) of the core leg divided by the number Nw of litz wires (1 , 2, 3, 4, 5, 6) of the conductor (125) is not an integer, and wherein the number of transpositions M provided in the winding arrangement (120) is one less than the number Nw of litz wires (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6) included in the group of litz wires (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6) of the conductor (125). The transformer (100) according to any one of the claims 1 to 8 and 10 to 16, wherein the number of turns Nt of the conductor (125) wound around the longitudinal axis (111) of the core leg divided by a prime factor P of the number Nw of litz wires (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6) of the conductor (125) is not an integer, and wherein the number of transpositions M provided in the winding arrangement (120) is one less than the number Nw of litz wires (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6) included in the group of litz wires (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6) of the conductor (125). The transformer (100) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein at least two transpositions (160), in which a radial position of the first subgroup of litz wires (1, 2, 3) and a radial position of the second subgroup of litz wires (4, 5, 6) are interchanged, are present at regular intervals of turns, in particular after an integer number of turns or after each turn, of the conductor (125) wound around the longitudinal axis (111). The transformer (100) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein each litz wire (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6) of the conductor (125) comprises many thin wire strands, which are individually insulated and twisted together, in particular wherein each litz wire (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6) includes an outer insulation. The transformer (100) according to claim 19, wherein the thin wire strands are grouped in bundles, are twisted together within each bundle, and the bundles are twisted together to form a single litz wire (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6); or the thin wire strands are grouped in sub-bundles and are twisted together within each sub- bundle, the sub-bundles are twisted together to form bundles, and the bundles are twisted together to form a single litz wire (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6). 21. The transformer (100) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the winding arrangement (120) consists of exactly one conductor (125) wound around the longitudinal axis (111) of the core leg with a number of turns Nt.
